# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std/http/server.ts";

const handler = async (req: Request): Promise<Response> => {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  let modulePath = url.searchParams.get("module") || Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATH");
  
  if (!modulePath) {
    return new Response("No module path provided", { status: 400 });
  }

  try {
    const module = await import(modulePath);
    const result = module.default(); // Assumes module exports a default function
    return new Response(JSON.stringify(result), {
      status: 200,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  } catch (error) {
    return new Response(`Error loading module: ${error.message}`, { status: 500 });
  }
};

serve(handler);
